full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Identifying implicit assumptions in variational inference for DP mixtures
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What are the key assumptions made when using mean-field variational inference for a Dirichlet Process mixture model? I want assumptions specific to inference, not general ML assumptions like i.i.d. data.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Pointed to mean-field factorisation and finite truncation as the two primary inference-level assumptions; I then located these in Section 3 of the paper
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Confirmed in Section 3 opening paragraph of the paper: 'we make the mean field assumption with truncated stick-breaking representations'

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding the exponential family assumption for features in iSVM
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In iSVM (Zhu et al. ICML 2011), the feature distribution is assumed to be from an exponential family. What computational advantages does this give for the phi update in Eq.(11), and what breaks if the features are not from an exponential family?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that E[γ_t]^T x - E[A(γ_t)] has a closed form only for exponential families via the conjugate prior structure
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against the DP mixture of input features subsection in Section 2.3 and the base distribution definition

top_5_prompts.prompt: What are the key assumptions made when using mean-field variational inference for a Dirichlet Process mixture model? I want assumptions specific to inference, not general ML assumptions like i.i.d. data.
top_5_prompts.why_important: This directly informed Assumption 1 about mean-field factorisation — the most impactful assumption for understanding when iSVM's inference breaks down.

top_5_prompts.prompt: In iSVM (Zhu et al. ICML 2011), the feature distribution is assumed to be from an exponential family. What computational advantages does this give for the phi update in Eq.(11), and what breaks if the features are not from an exponential family?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This informed Assumption 2 about exponential family features — critical for understanding why the paper works on real-valued Flickr features but not on arbitrary mixed data.
